title: Alampur Navabrahma Temples
tagline: Alampur Navabrahma Temples — spiritual town of Telangana

overview.short: Alampuram Navabrahma Temples are a group of nine early Badami Chalukyan Hindu temples dated between the 7th and 9th centuries that are located at Alampuram (Hemalapuram) in Telanga
overview.description: Alampuram Navabrahma Temples are a group of nine early Badami Chalukyan Hindu temples dated between the 7th and 9th centuries that are located at Alampuram (Hemalapuram) in Telangana, India, near the meeting point of Tungabhadra River and Krishna River at the border of Andhra Pradesh. They are called Nava-Brahma temples though they are dedicated to Shiva. They exemplify early North Indian Nagara style architecture with cut rock as the building block. The temples of Alampur resemble the style of Pattadakal, Aihole style as they were Karnata Dravida, Vesara style native to Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Alampur Museum
topPlaces.description: Alampur ASI Museum is a museum located in the historical temple town of Alampur, Mahbubnagar in Telangana. Situated at the Navabrahma Temples it is maintained by Archaeological Survey of India.

topPlaces.name: Jogulamba Temple, Alampur
topPlaces.description: Jogulamba Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Jogulamba, a form of Shakti located in Alampuram, Telangana, India. The temple is one of the Maha Shakta pithas, a group of eighteen (Ashtadasa) temples considered the most significant shrines and pilgrimage destinations in Shaktism.

topPlaces.name: Alampuram (Hemalapuram)
topPlaces.description: Alampuram (Hemalapuram) is a town situated in Jogulamba Gadwal district in the Indian state of Telangana. Alampur is a popular Hindu pilgrimage site in Shaktism and is also home to the Navabrahma Temples, a group of nine temples dedicated to Shiva built in the seventh and eighth century CE. It is the meeting point of the rivers Tungabhadra and Krishna and is referred to as Dakshina Kasi and is also considered the western gateway to Srisailam.

topPlaces.name: Alampur Papanasi Temples
topPlaces.description: Alampuram Papanasi Temples are a group of twenty three Hindu temples dated between 9th- and 11th-century that have been relocated to the southwest of Alampuram village in Telangana. This cluster of mostly ruined temples are co-located near the meeting point of Tungabhadra River and Krishna River at the border of Andhra Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Tungabhadra River
topPlaces.description: The Tungabhadra River (Kannada: [t̪uŋɡɐbʱɐd̪rɐ]) starts and flows through the state of Karnataka, India, for most of its course, then through the borders of Telangana and Andhra Pradesh where it ultimately joins the Krishna River near Gudimalla and Murvakonda villages. The Tungabhadra derives its name from two streams, the Tunga, about 147 km (91 mi) long, and the Bhadra, about 178 km (111 mi) long, which rise in the Western Ghats (Sahyadri Mountains).

topPlaces.name: Jogulamba Barrage
topPlaces.description: Jogulamba Barrage is a proposed barrage across Krishna River with full pond level (FPL) 274m. It would be located at Veltoor village, Peddamandadi mandal, Wanaparthy district, Telangana, India.

items.note: Alampur ASI Museum is a museum located in the historical temple town of Alampur, Mahbubnagar in Telangana. Situated at the Navabrahma Temples it is maintained by Archaeological Survey of India.

Jogulamba Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Jogulamba, a form of Shakti located in Alampuram, Telangana, India. The temple is one of the Maha Shakta pithas, a group of eighteen (Ashtadasa) temples considered the most significant shrines and pilgrimage destinations in Shaktism.

Alampuram (Hemalapuram) is a town situated in Jogulamba Gadwal district in the Indian state of Telangana. Alampur is a popular Hindu pilgrimage site in Shaktism and is also home to the Navabrahma Temples, a group of nine temples dedicated to Shiva built in the seventh and eighth century CE. It is the meeting point of the rivers Tungabhadra and Krishna and is referred to as Dakshina Kasi and is also considered the western gateway to Srisailam.

Alampuram Papanasi Temples are a group of twenty three Hindu temples dated between 9th- and 11th-century that have been relocated to the southwest of Alampuram village in Telangana. This cluster of mostly ruined temples are co-located near the meeting point of Tungabhadra River and Krishna River at the border of Andhra Pradesh.

The Tungabhadra River (Kannada: [t̪uŋɡɐbʱɐd̪rɐ]) starts and flows through the state of Karnataka, India, for most of its course, then through the borders of Telangana and Andhra Pradesh where it ultimately joins the Krishna River near Gudimalla and Murvakonda villages. The Tungabhadra derives its name from two streams, the Tunga, about 147 km (91 mi) long, and the Bhadra, about 178 km (111 mi) long, which rise in the Western Ghats (Sahyadri Mountains).
Jogulamba Barrage is a proposed barrage across Krishna River with full pond level (FPL) 274m. It would be located at Veltoor village, Peddamandadi mandal, Wanaparthy district, Telangana, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Alampur Navabrahma Temples?
faq.a: The best time to visit Alampur Navabrahma Temples is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Alampur Navabrahma Temples?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kurnool Airport (20 km) and the nearest railway station is Kacheguda (Hyderabad) (173 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Alampur Navabrahma Temples?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Alampur Navabrahma Temples at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Alampur Navabrahma Temples?
faq.a: Stays in Alampur Navabrahma Temples start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Alampur Navabrahma Temples?
faq.a: The top attractions are Alampur Museum, Jogulamba Temple, Alampur, Alampuram (Hemalapuram), Alampur Papanasi Temples, Tungabhadra River.
